what_it_does: Records a channel-memory attachment as YAML frontmatter in a Markdown file, linking an attachment identifier to a board item via a board:// URI and noting the channel, attachment type, and creation time.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text
The added timestamp uses ISO 8601 with microsecond precision
standouts: A board:// URI is used as the target reference
The file is purely a structured metadata card with no narrative content
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter delimited by --- in a Markdown file
Timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
References an external entity with a custom URI scheme
notes: A minimal, timestamped channel-memory attachment record that points to board item adb53e8f.
questions: What content or state does board://item-adb53e8f represent?
Why was this attachment created at this specific time?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a metadata record of an attachment to a board item.
intention_signals: To preserve a durable, retrievable record that a channel-memory artifact is associated with a specific board item.
contribution: A structured, timestamped edge record that documents the existence and target of a channel-to-board attachment.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file linking dragnet-development item att-5b7e5fd1 to board item adb53e8f with attachment type board_items and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a board item is attached to a channel-memory artifact for traceability.
